title: 5.1.2 — Auto-pause at markers (CLI flag + detection)
description:
## Summary

Add `--pause-at-markers` / `-m` CLI flag that pauses playback when a marker boundary is crossed during `tick()`. Detection lives in the TUI layer (app.rs), not in Player::tick().

## Files to Modify

- `crates/speedrun/src/main.rs` — Add `-m, --pause-at-markers` to `Args` struct. Pass to `App::new()`.
- `crates/speedrun/src/app.rs`:
  - Change `App::new()` signature to `pub fn new(player: Player, show_controls: bool, pause_at_markers: bool)`. Add `pause_at_markers: bool` field to `App`.
  - Add a standalone helper function for testability:
    ```rust
    /// Find the first marker whose time falls in the half-open interval (prev_time, new_time].
    fn find_crossed_marker(markers: &[Marker], prev_time: f64, new_time: f64) -> Option<f64> {
        markers.iter()
            .filter(|m| m.time > prev_time && m.time <= new_time)
            .map(|m| m.time)
            .reduce(f64::min)  // first marker if multiple
    }
    ```
  - In the event loop `run()` (lines ~190-200), wrap the existing tick block:
    ```rust
    if self.player.is_playing() {
        let prev_time = self.player.current_time();  // NEW
        self.player.tick(dt);
        // NEW: auto-pause at markers
        if self.pause_at_markers {
            if let Some(marker_time) = find_crossed_marker(
                self.player.markers(), prev_time, self.player.current_time()
            ) {
                self.player.pause();
                // seek() restores exact terminal state at marker time, since
                // tick() may have processed events slightly past the marker.
                self.player.seek(marker_time);
                self.controls_force_show = true;
                self.controls_manually_hidden = false;
            }
        }
        needs_redraw = true;
    }
    ```
  - Note: `controls_force_show` and `controls_manually_hidden` are set here (not deferred to a later ticket) so auto-pause immediately shows the controls bar.
  - Update all call sites of `App::new()` in tests — pass `false` for `pause_at_markers` in existing tests.

## Design Notes

- Detection only fires when `pause_at_markers == true` AND `player.is_playing() == true`.
- The `seek()` after detection is intentional: `tick()` may have processed events past the marker. `seek()` restores the exact terminal state at the marker time. Add a code comment explaining this.
- Only triggers during continuous `tick()` playback. NOT on seek, step, or `[`/`]` navigation.
- Multiple markers between prev_time and new_time: pause at the first one encountered (lowest time).

## Tests

### `crates/speedrun/src/main.rs` (in existing `mod tests`)

Following the project convention (see `args_accepts_dash_as_file` test):

1. **`test_pause_at_markers_long_flag`** — `Args::try_parse_from(["speedrun", "--pause-at-markers", "file.cast"])` succeeds.
2. **`test_pause_at_markers_short_flag`** — `Args::try_parse_from(["speedrun", "-m", "file.cast"])` succeeds.

### `crates/speedrun/src/app.rs` (in existing `mod tests`)

3. **`test_find_crossed_marker_hit`** — Markers at [3.0, 7.0]. `find_crossed_marker(markers, 2.0, 4.0)` returns `Some(3.0)`.
4. **`test_find_crossed_marker_miss`** — Markers at [3.0, 7.0]. `find_crossed_marker(markers, 4.0, 6.0)` returns `None`.
5. **`test_find_crossed_marker_multiple`** — Markers at [3.0, 5.0, 7.0]. `find_crossed_marker(markers, 2.0, 8.0)` returns `Some(3.0)` (first/lowest).
6. **`test_find_crossed_marker_at_prev_time`** — Markers at [3.0]. `find_crossed_marker(markers, 3.0, 5.0)` returns `None` (strict less-than prevents re-trigger on resume).
7. **`test_find_crossed_marker_at_new_time`** — Markers at [3.0]. `find_crossed_marker(markers, 1.0, 3.0)` returns `Some(3.0)` (inclusive upper bound).
8. **`test_find_crossed_marker_empty`** — Empty markers. `find_crossed_marker(&[], 0.0, 5.0)` returns `None`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `speedrun --pause-at-markers file.cast` is accepted by CLI parser.
- [ ] `speedrun -m file.cast` short form works.
- [ ] During `tick()` playback, crossing a marker pauses playback at the marker's exact time.
- [ ] Controls bar becomes visible immediately on auto-pause.
- [ ] When multiple markers exist between prev_time and new_time, pauses at the first one.
- [ ] Seek (arrows), step (`.`/`,`), and `[`/`]` do NOT trigger auto-pause.
- [ ] `JumpToPercent`, `JumpToStart`, `JumpToEnd` do NOT trigger auto-pause.
- [ ] Without `--pause-at-markers`, markers are crossed silently (existing behavior).
- [ ] With flag but no markers in recording, playback runs normally.
- [ ] Existing tests updated with new `App::new()` signature.
- [ ] At least 8 new tests (2 CLI + 6 helper function).
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` passes.

title: Epic 7 — Text Search
description: Search for text across the entire recording timeline. Adds a search engine to speedrun-core (lazy forward/backward search with keyframe acceleration) and a full search TUI (input bar, n/N navigation, match highlighting). Two sequential tickets: core engine first, then TUI integration.

title: 7.1 — Search engine (speedrun-core)
description:
Add text search to `speedrun-core` that finds occurrences of a query string across the timeline of a recording.

## Files to Create/Modify

- `crates/speedrun-core/src/search.rs` — new module with `SearchHit` type and search implementation
- `crates/speedrun-core/src/lib.rs` — add `pub mod search;` and re-export `SearchHit`
- `crates/speedrun-core/src/player.rs` — add `search_forward()`, `search_backward()` methods to `Player`

The search logic (keyframe restoration, replay, screen scanning) lives in `search.rs`. The `Player` methods are thin wrappers that pass `&self.recording`, `&self.time_map`, and `&self.index` to the search module.

## Types (in search.rs)

```rust
/// A single search match at a specific point in time.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
pub struct SearchHit {
    /// Effective time when this text is visible on screen.
    pub time: f64,
    /// Physical screen row (0-indexed).
    pub row: usize,
    /// Physical screen column (0-indexed, in terminal columns not char indices).
    pub col: usize,
    /// Match length in terminal columns.
    pub length: usize,
}
```

## API (methods on Player in player.rs)

Lazy, direction-based search — don't compute all matches eagerly:

```rust
impl Player {
    /// Find the next occurrence of `query` after `from_time`.
    /// Wraps around to the beginning if no match is found before the end.
    /// Returns `None` if the query is not found anywhere in the recording.
    pub fn search_forward(&self, query: &str, from_time: f64) -> Option<SearchHit>;

    /// Find the previous occurrence of `query` before `from_time`.
    /// Wraps around to the end if no match is found before the start.
    /// Returns `None` if the query is not found anywhere in the recording.
    pub fn search_backward(&self, query: &str, from_time: f64) -> Option<SearchHit>;
}
```

These methods take `&self` (not `&mut self`) — search uses throwaway `Vt` instances and does not modify the player's current state.

## Algorithm

Full-replay search with keyframe acceleration. NOT "scan keyframes first, then narrow" — that misses text visible only between keyframes.

### search_forward(query, from_time)

1. If `query` is empty, return `None`.
2. Lowercase the query for case-insensitive comparison.
3. Find the keyframe at or before `from_time` via `self.index.keyframe_at(from_time)`.
4. Restore that keyframe's snapshot into a **throwaway** `Vt` (via `snapshot.restore()`).
5. Replay events from the keyframe's `event_index` forward through `self.recording.events`:
   - For each event, call `feed_event(&mut vt, event)` (from ticket 5.3, or inline the match).
   - After each **output** event whose effective time is `> from_time`, scan all lines of `vt.view()`:
     - For each line, call `line.text()`, lowercase it, search for the query substring.
     - If found, compute the column position (accounting for the match position in the text string — for ASCII this is 1:1 with columns; for wide chars, iterate `line.cells()` to map char index to column).
     - Return the first `SearchHit` found (top-to-bottom, left-to-right).
6. If no match found by end of recording, **wrap around**: restore keyframe at time 0, replay events from 0 up to `from_time`, scanning after each output event. If still no match, return `None`.

### search_backward(query, from_time)

1. If `query` is empty, return `None`.
2. Work backward through keyframe intervals. Start from the keyframe at or before `from_time`.
3. For the current keyframe interval: restore the keyframe, replay events forward, but only collect matches with effective time `< from_time`. Keep track of the **last** match found (latest time, then bottom-to-top, right-to-left).
4. If a match is found, return it.
5. If not, move to the previous keyframe interval and repeat.
6. If no match found after reaching the start, **wrap around**: scan from the end of the recording backward to `from_time`.
7. If still no match, return `None`.

## Search Behavior

- Case-insensitive by default (compare lowercased strings)
- Substring match only (no regex in v1)
- Search physical screen lines individually via `line.text()`. Words that wrap across physical lines won't be found — document this limitation in a doc comment.
- Column positions are in terminal columns (for ASCII, 1 char = 1 column; for wide chars, 1 char may = 2 columns)
- Overlapping matches: find the first/leftmost only
- Alternate buffer: search sees whatever buffer is active at each point in time (correct since `vt.view()` returns the active buffer)

## Performance

`search_forward` should complete within 1 second for a 10-minute recording. Full replay of a 10-minute recording takes <500ms (per existing benchmarks). For performance testing, use `testdata/real_session.cast`.

## Tests

All tests go in `crates/speedrun-core/src/search.rs` under `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`. **Create inline test recordings** as byte strings — do NOT depend on testdata files for unit tests (keeps tests self-contained and makes assertions precise):

```rust
fn make_recording(events: &str) -> Vec<u8> {
    let header = r#"{"version":2,"width":80,"height":24}"#;
    format!("{header}\n{events}").into_bytes()
}
```

### Required test cases (minimum 10 tests):

1. **`test_search_forward_basic`** — recording with `[1.0,"o","hello world"]`, search for "hello", verify returns `Some(SearchHit { time: 1.0, row: 0, col: 0, length: 5 })`
2. **`test_search_forward_no_match`** — search for "nonexistent", verify returns `None`
3. **`test_search_forward_case_insensitive`** — recording outputs "Hello World", search for "hello world", verify match found
4. **`test_search_forward_empty_query`** — search for "", verify returns `None`
5. **`test_search_forward_empty_recording`** — recording with 0 events, search for anything, verify returns `None`
6. **`test_search_forward_wraparound`** — recording with "aaa" at t=1 and "bbb" at t=5. Search forward for "aaa" from t=3.0. Should wrap around and find the match at t=1.0.
7. **`test_search_forward_mid_recording`** — recording with "aaa" at t=1, "bbb" at t=3, "ccc" at t=5. Search forward for "bbb" from t=0. Verify hit at t=3.
8. **`test_search_forward_multiple_on_screen`** — recording with `[1.0,"o","hello hello"]`, search for "hello" from t=0, verify first match is at col=0 (leftmost match wins)
9. **`test_search_forward_does_not_modify_player`** — load recording, seek to t=2, call search_forward, verify `player.current_time()` is still 2.0 and screen is unchanged
10. **`test_search_backward_basic`** — recording with "aaa" at t=1, "bbb" at t=3. Search backward for "aaa" from t=5.0. Verify hit at t=1.0.
11. **`test_search_backward_wraparound`** — recording with "aaa" at t=1, "bbb" at t=3. Search backward for "bbb" from t=0.5. Should wrap around to find "bbb" at t=3.0.
12. **`test_search_between_keyframes`** — recording with events spanning > 5s (keyframe interval). Place text at an event between two keyframes. Search for that text. Verify it's found even though it doesn't appear at a keyframe boundary.

Also add one integration-level test using `testdata/real_session.cast` to verify search completes within 1 second.

## Verification

```bash
cargo test -p speedrun-core
cargo test --workspace
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- `search_forward("hello", 0.0)` on a recording containing "hello" returns `Some(SearchHit)` with correct time, row, col
- `search_forward("nonexistent", 0.0)` returns `None`
- Search is case-insensitive: query "HELLO" finds "hello" on screen
- Text that appears only between keyframes (not at any keyframe boundary) is found
- Empty query returns `None`
- Search on an empty recording (0 events) returns `None` without error
- Wraparound works: searching forward from the last match wraps to the first match
- Search does not modify `player.current_time()` or `player.screen()` state (uses throwaway Vt)
- `search_forward` completes within 1 second on `testdata/real_session.cast`
- At least 12 unit tests covering the scenarios listed above

title: SUPERSEDED — See hkp.2 and hkp.3
description:
Add search UI to the TUI player: input bar, navigation, match highlighting, and help overlay updates.

## Modal Input System

Add an `InputMode` enum to `App`:

```rust
enum InputMode {
    Normal,        // All keys go through map_key_event
    SearchInput,   // Keys are captured for the search query text field
}
```

When `InputMode::SearchInput` is active, all keys go to the search input handler — `Space` inserts a space (not toggle playback), `q` inserts 'q' (not quit), etc. Only `Enter` (execute search) and `Esc` (cancel search input) are handled specially. This is a structural change to the input pipeline — `map_key_event` is only called when `InputMode::Normal`.

## New Actions

Add to the `Action` enum:
- `StartSearch` — mapped to `/` in normal mode
- `NextMatch` — mapped to `n` in normal mode
- `PrevMatch` — mapped to `N` (Shift+n) in normal mode

## Search Input Bar

Rendered at the bottom of the screen (below or overlaying the controls bar). Shows `/ ` followed by the query text and a cursor. Supports: character insertion, `Backspace` to delete, `Enter` to execute, `Esc` to cancel. No cursor movement within the query (type + backspace only for v1). Long queries that exceed terminal width are truncated in display.

## Search State in App

- `search_query: Option<String>` — current active query (None = no search)
- `search_matches: Vec<SearchHit>` — cached results for current screen position
- `current_match_index: Option<usize>` — which match is "current" for n/N navigation

## Esc Behavior Chain

1. If `InputMode::SearchInput` -> cancel search input, return to Normal mode, keep any previous search results
2. If help overlay is visible -> dismiss help (existing behavior)
3. If search results are active (`search_query.is_some()`) -> clear search state
4. Otherwise -> quit

## n/N Navigation

- `n` calls `player.search_forward(query, current_time)` and seeks to the result
- `N` calls `player.search_backward(query, current_time)` and seeks to the result
- Wraps around (search functions handle wraparound)
- If no matches exist, show a brief "No matches" indicator (flash the search bar text or controls bar)
- `n`/`N` with no active search are ignored (no-op)

## Controls Bar Match Indicator

When search is active, show match count in the controls bar (e.g., `[3/17]`). Layout priority order (existing tiers apply): time -> progress bar -> match indicator -> speed. Match indicator is dropped before speed at narrow widths.

## Highlighting

When search is active and the current screen contains matching text, highlight matched cells in `TerminalView` with a distinct style. Implementation: after rendering the normal cell content, iterate visible SearchHits at the current time, apply a highlight modifier (e.g., `Modifier::REVERSED` combined with a yellow foreground, or just `Modifier::REVERSED` if the cell already has custom colors). Highlight ALL matches visible on screen, not just the "current" one. The "current" match (from n/N navigation) gets a brighter/different highlight to distinguish it.

Known limitation: highlight on cells with existing `REVERSED` modifier may be invisible. Accept this for v1.

## Search Interaction with Other UI States

- `/` while help overlay is open: ignored
- `?` while search input is open: ignored
- Play/pause while search is active: search results persist, highlighting updates per frame
- Manual seek while search active: results persist, current match index resets
- Stepping while search active: results persist, highlighting updates

## Viewport Scrolling

When `n`/`N` seeks to a match, if the match row is outside the visible viewport (host terminal smaller than recording), adjust `ViewportState` scroll offset to center the match row vertically.

## Help Overlay Update

Add a "Search" category to the help overlay listing `/`, `n`, `N`, `Esc` (clear search).

## Acceptance Criteria

- `/` opens search input bar at the bottom of the screen
- Typing in search input inserts characters; `Space` inserts a space (does not toggle playback); `q` inserts 'q' (does not quit)
- `Backspace` deletes the last character; `Enter` executes search; `Esc` cancels input
- After search, `n` seeks to the next match, `N` seeks to the previous match
- Wraparound: `n` past the last match wraps to the first match
- Zero matches: visible "no matches" feedback (not silent)
- Matched text on screen is visually highlighted with a distinct style
- The "current" match (jumped to via `n`/`N`) has a different highlight than other on-screen matches
- Controls bar shows match count indicator when search is active
- Help overlay includes Search keybinding category
- `Esc` chain works: cancel input -> dismiss help -> clear search -> quit
- Viewport scrolls to show off-screen matches when navigating with `n`/`N`
- Search state persists across play/pause/seek; clears on `Esc` (when no input/help is active)
- `n`/`N` with no active search are no-ops
- All existing tests pass; new tests cover input mode transitions, search state management, Esc chain, n/N navigation

title: 7.2a — Search input and navigation
description:
Add search input and navigation to the TUI player. This ticket covers the modal input system, search execution, and n/N match navigation. Highlighting and controls bar updates are in ticket 7.2b.

## Files to Modify

- `crates/speedrun/src/input.rs` — add `StartSearch`, `NextMatch`, `PrevMatch`, `Escape` actions and key mappings
- `crates/speedrun/src/app.rs` — add `InputMode` enum, search state fields, search input handling, Esc chain, n/N dispatch
- `crates/speedrun/src/main.rs` — no changes expected

## Modal Input System

Add an `InputMode` enum to `App`:

```rust
enum InputMode {
    Normal,        // All keys go through map_key_event
    SearchInput,   // Keys are captured for the search query text field
}
```

When `InputMode::SearchInput` is active, all keys go to a separate search input handler — `Space` inserts a space (not toggle playback), `q` inserts 'q' (not quit), etc. Only `Enter` (execute search) and `Esc` (cancel search input) are handled specially. `map_key_event` is only called when `InputMode::Normal`.

## New Actions in input.rs

Add to the `Action` enum:
- `StartSearch` — mapped to `KeyCode::Char('/')` in normal mode
- `NextMatch` — mapped to `KeyCode::Char('n')` in normal mode
- `PrevMatch` — mapped to `KeyCode::Char('N')` in normal mode (note: crossterm reports Shift+n as `KeyCode::Char('N')`, no modifier check needed)
- `Escape` — remap `KeyCode::Esc` from `Action::Quit` to `Action::Escape`. The Esc chain logic moves into `handle_action` in `app.rs`.

## Esc Behavior Chain (in app.rs handle_action)

When `Action::Escape` is received:
1. If `InputMode::SearchInput` -> cancel search input, return to Normal mode, keep any previous search results
2. If help overlay is visible -> dismiss help (existing behavior, currently on `Action::Quit` for Esc)
3. If search results are active (`search_query.is_some()`) -> clear search state
4. Otherwise -> quit (set `should_quit = true`)

## Search State in App

Add these fields to `App`:
- `input_mode: InputMode` — default `Normal`
- `search_input: String` — buffer for text being typed in search bar
- `search_query: Option<String>` — committed query (set on Enter)
- `current_match_index: Option<usize>` — for n/N navigation ordering

## Search Input Bar Rendering

Render a single-row bar at the bottom of the screen when `InputMode::SearchInput`. Shows `/ ` followed by the query text and a block cursor character. Supports: character insertion, `Backspace` to delete last char, `Enter` to execute, `Esc` to cancel. No cursor movement within the query (type + backspace only for v1). Long queries that exceed terminal width are truncated in display.

Render this in `app.rs`'s render method, below (or overlaying) the controls bar.

## n/N Navigation

- `n` calls `player.search_forward(query, current_time + epsilon)` and seeks to the result. The `+ epsilon` ensures we advance past the current match.
- `N` calls `player.search_backward(query, current_time - epsilon)` and seeks to the result.
- Wraps around (search functions handle wraparound internally).
- If `search_forward`/`search_backward` returns `None`, show feedback (e.g., set a transient "No matches" state that renders for 2 seconds).
- `n`/`N` with no active search (`search_query.is_none()`) are no-ops.

## Search Interaction with Other UI States

- `/` while help overlay is open: ignored
- `?` while search input is open: ignored (keys go to search handler)
- Play/pause while search is active: search results persist
- Manual seek while search active: results persist, current match index resets

## Tests

### Key mapping tests (in `input.rs` tests, follow existing pattern):

1. **`test_slash_maps_to_start_search`** — `map_key_event(key(KeyCode::Char('/')))` returns `Some(Action::StartSearch)`
2. **`test_n_maps_to_next_match`** — `map_key_event(key(KeyCode::Char('n')))` returns `Some(Action::NextMatch)`
3. **`test_shift_n_maps_to_prev_match`** — `map_key_event(key(KeyCode::Char('N')))` returns `Some(Action::PrevMatch)`
4. **`test_esc_maps_to_escape`** — `map_key_event(key(KeyCode::Esc))` returns `Some(Action::Escape)` (NOT `Action::Quit`)

### App state tests (in `app.rs` tests, follow existing pattern):

5. **`test_start_search_sets_input_mode`** — dispatch `StartSearch`, verify `input_mode == SearchInput`
6. **`test_esc_cancels_search_input`** — enter search mode, dispatch `Escape`, verify `input_mode == Normal`
7. **`test_esc_dismisses_help`** — open help, dispatch `Escape`, verify help is dismissed
8. **`test_esc_clears_search`** — set a search query, dispatch `Escape`, verify `search_query == None`
9. **`test_esc_quits_when_nothing_active`** — no search, no help, dispatch `Escape`, verify `should_quit == true`
10. **`test_esc_chain_order`** — verify the priority: search input mode > help overlay > search active > quit
11. **`test_next_match_no_search_is_noop`** — dispatch `NextMatch` with no search query, verify no state change
12. **`test_start_search_ignored_during_help`** — show help, dispatch `StartSearch`, verify still in Normal mode with help showing

## Verification

```bash
cargo test --workspace
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- `/` opens search input bar at the bottom of the screen
- Typing in search input inserts characters; `Space` inserts a space (does not toggle playback); `q` inserts 'q' (does not quit)
- `Backspace` deletes the last character; `Enter` executes search; `Esc` cancels input
- After search, `n` seeks to the next match, `N` seeks to the previous match
- Wraparound: `n` past the last match wraps to the first match
- Zero matches: visible "No matches" feedback (not silent)
- `Esc` chain works: cancel input -> dismiss help -> clear search -> quit (test each stage)
- Search state persists across play/pause/seek; clears on `Esc` (when no input/help is active)
- `n`/`N` with no active search are no-ops
- At least 12 new tests covering key mappings, app state transitions, and Esc chain
- All existing tests pass

title: 7.2b — Search highlighting and UI updates
description:
Add search highlighting, controls bar match indicator, help overlay updates, and viewport scrolling for search matches. Depends on ticket 7.2a (search input and navigation must be working).

## Files to Modify

- `crates/speedrun/src/ui.rs` — add match highlighting to `TerminalView` widget
- `crates/speedrun/src/controls.rs` — add match count indicator to controls bar
- `crates/speedrun/src/help.rs` — add Search keybinding category
- `crates/speedrun/src/app.rs` — viewport scroll adjustment on n/N, pass search state to widgets

## Highlighting in TerminalView

When search is active and the current screen contains matching text, highlight matched cells. Implementation:

1. `TerminalView` receives an optional list of on-screen matches: `matches: &[(usize, usize, usize)]` as `(row, col, length)` tuples, plus an optional `current_match: Option<usize>` index.
2. After rendering normal cell content, iterate the match list. For each match, apply a highlight style to cells at `(row, col..col+length)`:
   - Non-current matches: `Modifier::REVERSED` (swaps fg/bg for visibility)
   - Current match (the one jumped to via n/N): yellow background (`Color::Indexed(11)`) with black foreground
3. Only highlight matches that fall within the visible viewport area.

Known limitation: highlight on cells with existing `REVERSED` modifier may be invisible. Accept this for v1.

## Finding On-Screen Matches

In `app.rs`, before rendering, compute which search hits are visible on the current screen:
1. If `search_query` is active, iterate physical screen lines (`player.screen()`) and search each line's `.text()` for the query (case-insensitive).
2. Collect `(row, col, length)` tuples for all matches on the current screen.
3. Pass these to `TerminalView` for highlighting.

This is an O(rows * line_length) operation per render — acceptable for typical terminal sizes.

## Controls Bar Match Indicator

When search is active, show a match indicator in the controls bar. Use the format `[query]` (showing what was searched). Do NOT show a total count (the search API is lazy and computing total count requires full replay).

Layout priority order: time -> progress bar -> search indicator -> speed. Search indicator is dropped before speed at narrow widths. Add a new responsive layout tier if needed.

## Help Overlay Update

Add a "Search" category to the help overlay keybinding groups:

```
Search
  /         search
  n         next match
  N         previous match
  Esc       clear search
```

## Viewport Scrolling for Off-Screen Matches

When `n`/`N` navigation in 7.2a seeks to a match, if the match row is outside the visible viewport (host terminal smaller than recording), adjust `ViewportState` scroll offset to center the match row vertically. Modify the n/N handler in `app.rs` to update `viewport.scroll_y` based on the `SearchHit.row` after seeking.

## Tests

Tests go in `crates/speedrun/src/ui.rs` (for highlight positioning), `crates/speedrun/src/controls.rs` (for indicator rendering), and `crates/speedrun/src/help.rs` (for help content). Use `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in each file.

### Required test cases (minimum 8 tests):

#### Highlight positioning tests (in ui.rs)

1. **`test_highlight_positions_basic`** — Given a screen with "hello world" at row 0 and search query "hello", verify the computed match list contains `(0, 0, 5)`.
2. **`test_highlight_positions_multiple_per_line`** — Screen with "abcabc" at row 0, search for "abc", verify two matches: `(0, 0, 3)` and `(0, 3, 3)`.
3. **`test_highlight_positions_multiple_lines`** — Screen with "foo" at row 0 and "bar foo" at row 2, search for "foo", verify matches at `(0, 0, 3)` and `(2, 4, 3)`.
4. **`test_highlight_positions_case_insensitive`** — Screen with "Hello" at row 0, search for "hello", verify match found.
5. **`test_highlight_positions_no_match`** — Screen with "hello" at row 0, search for "xyz", verify empty match list.

Note: Extract the match-finding logic into a standalone function `fn find_on_screen_matches(lines: &[impl AsRef<str>], query: &str) -> Vec<(usize, usize, usize)>` in `ui.rs` (or a shared helper module) so it can be unit-tested independently of the full widget render pipeline.

#### Controls bar tests (in controls.rs)

6. **`test_controls_bar_search_indicator`** — Use `insta::assert_snapshot!` to snapshot the controls bar output when search is active with query "test". Verify the snapshot contains `[test]`. Compare against the same controls bar without search active.
7. **`test_controls_bar_search_indicator_narrow_width`** — Render the controls bar at a narrow width (e.g., 40 columns) with search active. Verify the search indicator is dropped before the speed indicator (layout priority order: time > progress > search > speed).

#### Help overlay tests (in help.rs)

8. **`test_help_overlay_search_category`** — Use `insta::assert_snapshot!` to snapshot the full help overlay content. Verify the snapshot contains the "Search" category with the keybindings `/`, `n`, `N`, and `Esc`.

### Insta snapshot guidance

For controls bar and help overlay tests, use `insta::assert_snapshot!` to capture the rendered widget output. To render a widget to a string for snapshotting:

```rust
use ratatui::prelude::*;
use ratatui::backend::TestBackend;
use ratatui::Terminal;

fn render_to_string(widget: impl Widget, width: u16, height: u16) -> String {
    let backend = TestBackend::new(width, height);
    let mut terminal = Terminal::new(backend).unwrap();
    terminal.draw(|frame| {
        frame.render_widget(widget, frame.area());
    }).unwrap();
    let buffer = terminal.backend().buffer().clone();
    // Convert buffer to string for snapshot
    let mut output = String::new();
    for y in 0..buffer.area.height {
        for x in 0..buffer.area.width {
            let cell = &buffer[(x, y)];
            output.push_str(cell.symbol());
        }
        output.push('\n');
    }
    output
}
```

Review how existing tests in `controls.rs` and `help.rs` render widgets — follow the same pattern. If existing tests already use `insta`, follow the same approach.

## Verification

```bash
cargo test --workspace
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Matched text on screen is visually highlighted with a distinct style
- The "current" match (jumped to via n/N) has a different highlight than other on-screen matches
- Controls bar shows search query indicator when search is active
- Help overlay includes Search keybinding category with /, n, N, Esc
- Viewport scrolls to show off-screen matches when navigating with n/N
- Highlighting updates correctly when playback advances (new frame may have different matches)
- All existing tests pass; at least 8 new tests covering: highlight position calculation, controls bar with search indicator, help overlay content

title: 3.0 — Action enum and input module (Phase 3 foundation)
description:
## Summary

Create `input.rs` with a full `Action` enum and key-event-to-action mapping function. Refactor the inline `handle_key` in `app.rs` to use action-based dispatch. This is the foundation ticket for Phase 3 — all three parallel epics (controls bar, navigation/speed, help overlay) build on this.

## Context

Key handling is currently inline in `app.rs::handle_key()` (lines 89-117) with a direct `match key.code` that handles only Space, q/Esc, Ctrl+c, and arrow keys. There is no `input.rs` module and no `Action` enum. Phase 3 adds ~15 new keybindings across three parallel epics. Without a shared Action enum merged first, all three epics would create conflicting structural changes to `app.rs`.

## Files

- `crates/speedrun/src/input.rs` — NEW file
- `crates/speedrun/src/app.rs` — refactor `handle_key`, add `handle_action` dispatcher
- `crates/speedrun/src/main.rs` — add `mod input;`

## Tasks

1. **Create `input.rs` with the `Action` enum.** Define all variants needed for Phase 3:
   - `Quit`
   - `TogglePlayback`
   - `SeekForward` (5s)
   - `SeekBackward` (5s)
   - `SeekForward30s`
   - `SeekBackward30s`
   - `StepForward`
   - `StepBackward`
   - `SpeedUp`
   - `SpeedDown`
   - `NextMarker`
   - `PrevMarker`
   - `JumpToPercent(u8)` (0-9 → 0%-90%)
   - `JumpToStart`
   - `JumpToEnd`
   - `ToggleControls`
   - `ToggleHelp`

2. **Implement `map_key_event(KeyEvent) -> Option<Action>`.** Map all keybindings from the spec:
   - `q` → Quit
   - `Esc` → Quit (dispatch layer will intercept when help is visible — that is NOT this ticket's concern)
   - `Ctrl+c` → Quit
   - `Space` → TogglePlayback
   - `Right` → SeekForward
   - `Left` → SeekBackward
   - `Shift+Right` → SeekForward30s
   - `Shift+Left` → SeekBackward30s
   - `.` → StepForward
   - `,` → StepBackward
   - `+` or `=` → SpeedUp
   - `-` → SpeedDown
   - `]` → NextMarker
   - `[` → PrevMarker
   - `0`-`9` → JumpToPercent(digit)
   - `Home` or `g` → JumpToStart
   - `End` or `G` (Shift+g) → JumpToEnd
   - `Tab` → ToggleControls
   - `?` (Shift+/) → ToggleHelp
   - All other keys → None (ignored)

   Note: filter `key.kind != KeyEventKind::Press` before calling this function (keep that in the caller).

3. **Refactor `app.rs`.** Split `handle_key` into two methods:
   - `handle_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent)` — filters non-Press events, updates `last_interaction`, calls `map_key_event`, dispatches to `handle_action`.
   - `handle_action(&mut self, action: Action)` — match on Action. Implement existing actions (Quit, TogglePlayback, SeekForward, SeekBackward). All new actions get no-op arms with `// TODO: implemented in Phase 3 epic` comments for now.

4. **Wire module.** Add `pub mod input;` to `main.rs` (matching existing `pub mod ui;` pattern). Import `Action` and `map_key_event` in `app.rs`.

5. **Write unit tests in `input.rs`.** Test every key mapping:
   - Each keybinding produces the correct Action
   - Modifier combos work (Shift+Right → SeekForward30s, not SeekForward)
   - Unmapped keys (e.g., `x`, `F1`) return None
   - Digit keys 0-9 map to correct JumpToPercent values
   - `G` (Shift+g) → JumpToEnd, `g` (no shift) → JumpToStart

## Important: ratatui/crossterm version notes

This project uses crossterm 0.27. Key events include `kind` field — only `KeyEventKind::Press` should be handled (release events are filtered). Shift+letter keys: crossterm reports `KeyCode::Char('G')` with `KeyModifiers::SHIFT` for Shift+g.

**KeyEvent construction in tests:**
```rust
use crossterm::event::{KeyCode, KeyEvent, KeyModifiers};

// Simple key (Press event by default):
KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Char('q'), KeyModifiers::NONE)

// With modifiers:
KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Right, KeyModifiers::SHIFT)  // Shift+Right
KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Char('c'), KeyModifiers::CONTROL)  // Ctrl+c
KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Char('G'), KeyModifiers::SHIFT)  // Shift+g (uppercase G)

// Special keys:
KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Esc, KeyModifiers::NONE)
KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Tab, KeyModifiers::NONE)
KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Home, KeyModifiers::NONE)
KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::End, KeyModifiers::NONE)
```

Test pattern: call `map_key_event(key_event)` and assert on the returned `Option<Action>`:
```rust
assert_eq!(map_key_event(KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Char('q'), KeyModifiers::NONE)), Some(Action::Quit));
assert_eq!(map_key_event(KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Char('x'), KeyModifiers::NONE)), None);
```
Derive `Debug, PartialEq` on `Action` to enable `assert_eq!` comparisons.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `crates/speedrun/src/input.rs` exists with `Action` enum containing all 17 variants listed above
- [ ] `map_key_event` handles all keybindings from the spec and returns `Option<Action>`
- [ ] `app.rs` `handle_key` delegates to `map_key_event` → `handle_action`
- [ ] Existing behavior is preserved: Space toggles, q/Esc quits, arrows seek ±5s, Ctrl+c quits
- [ ] `last_interaction` is still updated on every keypress
- [ ] `show_controls = true` is still set on seek actions
- [ ] New actions (SpeedUp, StepForward, etc.) are matched but are no-ops for now
- [ ] Unit tests cover all key mappings including edge cases (Shift+arrows, digit keys, unmapped keys)
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace` passes with zero warnings
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` passes
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes

title: Epic 1.5 — Player
description:
The main public API for speedrun-core. The Player owns the Recording, TimeMap, KeyframeIndex, and current Vt instance. It provides loading, seeking, continuous playback, and single-step navigation.

## Context

This is the capstone of the core engine — it integrates all previous modules into a single API that the TUI (and future WASM player) consumes. The spec defines the full public API surface in SPEC.md lines 169–248.

### Key design decisions

- **`PlayerError`** wraps `ParseError` and `TimeMapError` (the two fallible operations during loading).
- **Idle limit resolution** precedence: `LoadOptions.idle_limit` > header `idle_time_limit` > no limit.
- The Player owns a `Vt` instance that is **rebuilt on every seek** (restored from keyframe snapshot + event replay) and **mutated in-place during tick/step**.
- **`screen()` returns `&[avt::Line]`** (via `vt.view()`). The spec says `&avt::Screen` but avt does not export a `Screen` type; `&[Line]` is the actual API.
- **Marker times** are converted from raw to effective at load time.
- **`step_backward()`** is internally a seek (avt is forward-only, so we must replay from a keyframe).
- **Auto-pause at end**: `tick()` returns `true` once for the final frame, `false` on subsequent calls.

### avt API surface (verified)

- `Vt::view() -> &[Line]` — line grid for rendering
- `Vt::cursor() -> Cursor` — cursor with `col`, `row`, `visible` fields
- `Vt::size() -> (usize, usize)` — (cols, rows)
- `Vt::feed_str(&mut self, s: &str) -> (Vec<usize>, bool)` — feed data, returns dirty lines (ignored)
- `Vt::dump() -> String` / `TerminalSnapshot::capture/restore` — state serialization (used by keyframe index)

## Scope

- `crates/speedrun-core/src/player.rs` (new)
- Updates to `lib.rs`

## Tickets

- 1.5.1 — Player struct, load, seek, and screen access
- 1.5.2 — Playback control, tick, and stepping
- 1.5.3 — Player tests

title: 1.5.1 — Player struct, load, seek, and screen access
description:
Define the Player struct, error types, load pipeline, seek engine, and state accessors. This is the foundation that all playback control builds on.

## Tasks

1. Create `crates/speedrun-core/src/player.rs` with error type:

   ```rust
   #[derive(Debug)]
   pub enum PlayerError {
       Parse(ParseError),
       TimeMap(TimeMapError),
   }
   ```

   Implement `std::fmt::Display`, `std::error::Error`, `From<ParseError>`, and `From<TimeMapError>`.

2. Define `LoadOptions`:

   ```rust
   pub struct LoadOptions {
       /// Cap idle time between events (seconds).
       /// `Some(limit)` overrides the header's `idle_time_limit`.
       /// `None` means use the header value, or no limit if the header
       /// doesn't specify one either.
       pub idle_limit: Option<f64>,
   }

   impl Default for LoadOptions {
       fn default() -> Self { Self { idle_limit: None } }
   }
   ```

3. Define `Player` struct (fields are private):

   ```rust
   pub struct Player {
       recording: Recording,
       time_map: TimeMap,
       index: KeyframeIndex,
       vt: avt::Vt,
       /// Current position in effective time.
       current_time: f64,
       /// Index of the next event to process (into recording.events).
       current_event_index: usize,
       /// Markers with effective times (converted at load).
       markers: Vec<Marker>,
       /// Whether playback is active.
       playing: bool,
       /// Playback speed multiplier.
       speed: f64,
   }
   ```

4. Implement `Player::load(reader: impl Read) -> Result<Self, PlayerError>`:
   - Delegates to `load_with(reader, LoadOptions::default())`.

5. Implement `Player::load_with(reader: impl Read, opts: LoadOptions) -> Result<Self, PlayerError>`:
   - Parse recording: `parse(reader)?`
   - Resolve idle limit: `opts.idle_limit.or(recording.header.idle_time_limit)` — CLI override > header > None (no limit).
   - Extract raw timestamps: `recording.events.iter().map(|e| e.time).collect()`
   - Build time map: `TimeMap::build(&raw_times, resolved_limit)?`
   - Build keyframe index: `KeyframeIndex::build(&recording, &time_map)`
   - Create initial Vt: `create_vt(header.width as usize, header.height as usize)`
   - Convert markers to effective time: iterate `recording.events` by index, for each `EventType::Marker`, look up `time_map.effective_time(i)` and build a `Marker { time: effective_time, label }`.
   - Initialize state: `current_time = 0.0`, `current_event_index = 0`, `playing = false`, `speed = 1.0`.

6. Implement the internal replay method `fn replay_to(&mut self, target_time: f64)`:
   - Find nearest keyframe: `self.index.keyframe_at(target_time)`.
   - **If found**: get the keyframe, restore Vt via `keyframe.snapshot.restore()`, set `self.current_event_index = keyframe.event_index`.
   - **If not found** (target before first keyframe, or empty index): create a fresh Vt via `create_vt(header.width, header.height)`, set `self.current_event_index = 0`.
   - Replace `self.vt` with the restored/fresh Vt.
   - Feed events forward from `current_event_index`:
     ```rust
     while self.current_event_index < self.recording.events.len() {
         let Some(t) = self.time_map.effective_time(self.current_event_index) else { break };
         if t > target_time { break; }
         match &self.recording.events[self.current_event_index] {
             // Output: vt.feed_str(&data)
             // Resize: vt.feed_str(&format!("\x1b[8;{rows};{cols}t"))
             // Input/Marker: skip
         }
         self.current_event_index += 1;
     }
     ```
     Note: the condition is `t > target_time` (i.e., include events AT target_time). After the loop, `current_event_index` points to the first event NOT yet processed.
   - Set `self.current_time = target_time`.

7. Implement `seek(&mut self, time: f64)`:
   - Clamp `time` to `[0.0, self.duration()]`.
   - Call `self.replay_to(clamped_time)`.

8. Implement `seek_relative(&mut self, delta: f64)`:
   - `self.seek(self.current_time + delta)`.

9. Implement accessors:
   - `screen(&self) -> &[avt::Line]` — returns `self.vt.view()`
   - `cursor(&self) -> CursorState` — returns `CursorState::from_vt(&self.vt)`
   - `size(&self) -> (u16, u16)` — `let (c, r) = self.vt.size(); (c as u16, r as u16)`
   - `current_time(&self) -> f64`
   - `duration(&self) -> f64` — returns `self.time_map.duration()`
   - `markers(&self) -> &[Marker]` — returns `&self.markers`

10. Add `pub mod player;` to `lib.rs`. Re-export: `pub use player::{Player, PlayerError, LoadOptions};`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `PlayerError` with `Display`/`Error`/`From` impls for both inner error types
- [ ] `LoadOptions` with `Default` impl
- [ ] `Player::load()` and `load_with()` successfully load all valid test data files
- [ ] Idle limit resolution: CLI override > header `idle_time_limit` > no limit
- [ ] `seek()` clamps to [0, duration] and restores correct terminal state
- [ ] `seek_relative()` works for positive and negative deltas
- [ ] `screen()` returns `&[avt::Line]` from the current Vt view
- [ ] `cursor()` returns correct `CursorState`
- [ ] `size()` returns current Vt dimensions as `(u16, u16)`
- [ ] `duration()`, `current_time()`, `markers()` return correct values
- [ ] Marker times are effective (not raw)
- [ ] Module added to `lib.rs` with re-exports
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` passes
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes
notes:
## Codebase context

**avt API (verified from source):**
- `Vt::view() -> &[avt::Line]` — no `avt::Screen` type exists. Adapt the spec's `screen() -> &avt::Screen` accordingly.
- `Vt::size() -> (usize, usize)` — returns `(cols, rows)`, cast to `u16` for public API.
- `Vt::cursor() -> avt::Cursor` — has `.col`, `.row`, `.visible` fields. `CursorState::from_vt()` already extracts these.
- `Vt::feed_str() -> (Vec<usize>, bool)` — ignore the return value for playback.

**Resize parameter order**: `EventData::Resize { cols, rows }` but xtwinops is `\x1b[8;{rows};{cols}t`. Same pattern as in the keyframe indexer.

**Marker conversion**: `Recording.markers` has raw times. Iterate `recording.events` by index to find `EventType::Marker` events and look up `time_map.effective_time(event_index)` for each. The parser ensures markers appear in both `events` and `markers` vecs, but the events vec gives us the index for TimeMap lookup.

**`replay_to()` event inclusion**: Events with `effective_time <= target_time` are included (fed to Vt). After replay, `current_event_index` points to the first event NOT yet processed. This ensures `tick()` (next ticket) doesn't double-process events.

## Verification commands

```bash
cargo build -p speedrun-core
cargo clippy -p speedrun-core -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check
```

title: 1.5.2 — Playback control, tick, and stepping
description:
Implement continuous playback advancement (tick), playback state control (play/pause/speed), frame timing for the event loop, and single-event stepping.

## Tasks

1. **Playback state control:**

   ```rust
   pub fn play(&mut self)          // sets playing = true
   pub fn pause(&mut self)         // sets playing = false
   pub fn toggle(&mut self)        // flips playing
   pub fn is_playing(&self) -> bool
   pub fn set_speed(&mut self, speed: f64)
   pub fn speed(&self) -> f64
   ```

2. **`tick(&mut self, dt: f64) -> bool`** — advance playback by `dt` wall-clock seconds:

   - If not playing, return `false` immediately.
   - Compute `new_time = self.current_time + dt * self.speed`.
   - Clamp `new_time` to `self.duration()`.
   - Track whether any events were processed (`state_changed = false`).
   - Feed events using the same loop pattern as `replay_to`:
     ```rust
     while self.current_event_index < self.recording.events.len() {
         let Some(t) = self.time_map.effective_time(self.current_event_index) else { break };
         if t > new_time { break; }
         // Output: feed to vt, state_changed = true
         // Resize: feed to vt, state_changed = true
         // Input/Marker: skip
         self.current_event_index += 1;
     }
     ```
   - Update `self.current_time = new_time`.
   - **Auto-pause at end**: if `new_time >= self.duration()`, set `self.playing = false` and `state_changed = true` (so the TUI re-renders the final frame with paused state).
   - Return `state_changed`.

3. **`time_to_next_event(&self) -> Option<std::time::Duration>`** — for the TUI event loop:

   - If not playing, return `None`.
   - If `current_event_index >= events.len()`, return `None` (at end).
   - Get the next event's effective time: `time_map.effective_time(current_event_index)`.
   - Compute wall-clock delta: `(next_effective_time - current_time) / speed`.
   - If delta <= 0 (event is overdue), return `Some(Duration::ZERO)`.
   - Convert positive delta to `Duration` and return `Some(duration)`.

4. **`step_forward(&mut self) -> bool`** — advance to the next output event:

   - If `self.playing`, return `false` (stepping only when paused).
   - Scan from `current_event_index` forward, looking for `EventType::Output`. Process each event as you scan:
     - `Resize`: feed to Vt (resizes affect Vt state and must not be skipped).
     - `Input` / `Marker`: skip (no Vt state change).
     - `Output`: feed to Vt, update `current_time` to this event's effective time, set `current_event_index` past it, return `true`.
   - If loop exhausts all events without finding an output event, return `false` (at end).

5. **`step_backward(&mut self) -> bool`** — seek to the previous output event:

   - If `self.playing`, return `false` (stepping only when paused).
   - **Guard against underflow**: if `current_event_index == 0`, return `false` (already at start, no previous event exists). Do NOT compute `current_event_index - 1` without this check — it panics on usize underflow.
   - Scan backward from `current_event_index - 1` toward 0, looking for `EventType::Output`.
   - If found: call `self.seek(effective_time_of_that_event)`. This rebuilds from a keyframe since avt is forward-only.
   - If no previous output event found, return `false`.
   - Return `true`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `play()`/`pause()`/`toggle()`/`is_playing()` work correctly
- [ ] `set_speed()`/`speed()` work correctly
- [ ] `tick()` advances time by `dt × speed`, feeds events to Vt, returns whether state changed
- [ ] `tick()` returns `false` when not playing
- [ ] `tick()` auto-pauses at end of recording, returns `true` for the final frame
- [ ] Subsequent `tick()` calls after reaching end return `false`
- [ ] `time_to_next_event()` returns correct wall-clock `Duration` accounting for speed
- [ ] `time_to_next_event()` returns `None` when paused or at end
- [ ] `step_forward()` advances to next output event, processing intervening resizes
- [ ] `step_forward()` skips input/marker events
- [ ] `step_forward()` returns `false` at end of recording
- [ ] `step_backward()` seeks to previous output event via keyframe replay
- [ ] `step_backward()` returns `false` at start of recording (including when `current_event_index == 0`)
- [ ] Both step methods return `false` when `is_playing()` is true
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` passes
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes
notes:
## Codebase context

**Depends on 1.5.1**: The `Player` struct, `replay_to()`, `seek()`, and all accessors already exist. This ticket adds playback advancement methods.

**`tick()` performance**: Called on every frame. The hot path processes only events in the `[current_time, new_time]` window — typically 1–3 events. `vt.feed_str()` handles these in microseconds.

**`step_backward()` cost**: Internally calls `seek()`, which restores from a keyframe and replays forward. Bounded by `KEYFRAME_INTERVAL` (5s of events) — still fast but more expensive than `step_forward()`.

**`time_to_next_event()` is the TUI's sleep duration**: The event loop calls `crossterm::event::poll(timeout)` with this value. Returning `None` means the loop uses a default idle poll interval (100ms). Returning `Duration::ZERO` means "there's an event ready right now."

**Event processing in `step_forward`**: Unlike `tick()` which skips non-output events silently, `step_forward` must process intervening resize events so the Vt dimensions stay correct. It steps through events one by one until it finds the next output event.

## Verification commands

```bash
cargo build -p speedrun-core
cargo clippy -p speedrun-core -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check
```

title: 1.5.3 — Player tests
description:
Comprehensive test suite for all Player behavior: loading, seeking, playback, stepping, and metadata.

## Tasks

1. **Load tests:**
   - Smoke test: load all valid test data files in a loop (`minimal_v2.cast`, `minimal_v3.cast`, `empty.cast`, `long_idle.cast`, `with_markers.cast`, `with_resize.cast`, `alternate_buffer.cast`, `real_session.cast`) — verify no error and non-panic. This is one test function, not eight.

   - Error cases: empty bytes and binary garbage — verify appropriate `PlayerError` variant.

2. **Seek tests** (including screen content verification):
   - `seek(0.0)` — verify cursor at origin, screen is blank (initial state).
   - `seek(known_event_time)` — load `minimal_v2.cast`, seek to the effective time of the last event, verify `screen()[0].text()` contains expected output.
   - `seek(between_events)` — verify state reflects events up to but not past target time.
   - `seek(duration + 100.0)` — clamps to duration.
   - `seek(-5.0)` — clamps to 0.
   - `seek_relative(+delta)` and `seek_relative(-delta)` — verify `current_time()` updates correctly.
   - Seek forward then backward — verify state is consistent (no accumulation artifacts from forward-only Vt).

3. **Tick tests:**
   - Start playing, `tick(small_dt)` — verify events are fed, `current_time()` advances, returns `true` if events were processed.
   - `tick(dt)` at 2× speed — verify `current_time()` advances by `2 * dt`.
   - `tick()` when paused — returns `false`, `current_time()` unchanged.
   - `tick()` that reaches end — auto-pauses (`is_playing() == false`), returns `true`. Subsequent `tick()` returns `false`.

4. **`time_to_next_event` tests:**
   - At start, playing at 1× — returns time to first event's effective time.
   - At 2× speed — returns half the time.
   - When paused or at end — returns `None`.

5. **Stepping tests:**
   - `step_forward()` from start while paused — advances to first output event, returns `true`.
   - `step_forward()` skips input/marker events (use `with_markers.cast`).
   - `step_forward()` at end — returns `false`.
   - `step_backward()` from mid-recording — goes to previous output event, verify `current_time()` reflects that event.
   - `step_backward()` at start — returns `false`.
   - Both step methods while playing — return `false`.

6. **Marker and insta snapshot tests:**
   - Load `with_markers.cast` — verify `markers()` returns 2 markers with correct labels and effective times (not raw).
   - Load `minimal_v2.cast`, seek to end, snapshot the screen text lines. Locks down the full pipeline: parse → time map → index → seek → render.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 6 test groups pass
- [ ] Load smoke test covers all valid files; specific tests for idle limit resolution and errors
- [ ] Seek accuracy verified with screen content checks
- [ ] Tick advancement and auto-pause verified
- [ ] Stepping verified for forward, backward, and boundary cases

title: Epic 2.2 — TerminalView Widget
description:
Implement the ratatui TerminalView widget that renders avt's terminal cell grid to the host terminal. This is a pure rendering component with no side effects — it maps avt cells to ratatui cells with correct colors, attributes, cursor rendering, and viewport scrolling.

## Context

The core engine (speedrun-core) provides `player.screen() -> &[avt::Line]` which gives access to the terminal cell grid. Each `avt::Line` contains `Cell(char, Pen)` tuples. The TerminalView widget must map these to ratatui buffer cells.

### Key design decisions

- **Cell-by-cell mapping** using `line.cells()` iterator which yields `(char, Pen)` pairs.
- **Color mapping** is direct: `avt::Color::Indexed(u8)` → `ratatui::Color::Indexed(u8)`, `avt::Color::RGB(RGB8)` → `ratatui::Color::Rgb(r, g, b)`, `None` → `Color::Reset`.
- **Attribute mapping**: `Pen` has `is_bold()`, `is_faint()`, `is_italic()`, `is_underline()`, `is_strikethrough()`, `is_blink()`, `is_inverse()` which map to ratatui `Modifier` flags.
- **Wide characters**: `char_width()` via `unicode-width` — wide chars occupy 2 cells, so the second cell must be skipped.
- **Cursor rendering**: if visible, apply `REVERSED` modifier at cursor position.
- **Viewport scrolling**: when host terminal is smaller than recording, the viewport follows the cursor.

### avt API surface (verified)

- `avt::Line::cells() -> impl Iterator<Item = (char, Pen)>` — cell-by-cell with character and style
- `avt::Cell(char, Pen)` — tuple struct, Cell is `pub(crate)` but accessed through `Line::cells()`
- `avt::Pen` — style information:
  - `foreground() -> Option<Color>`, `background() -> Option<Color>`
  - `is_bold()`, `is_faint()`, `is_italic()`, `is_underline()`, `is_strikethrough()`, `is_blink()`, `is_inverse()`
- `avt::Color` — `Indexed(u8) | RGB(rgb::RGB8)` where `RGB8 { r: u8, g: u8, b: u8 }`
- `avt::Vt::view() -> &[Line]` — the visible line grid
- `avt::Vt::cursor() -> Cursor` — `{ col: usize, row: usize, visible: bool }`

### ratatui API surface (verified)

- `Widget` trait: `fn render(self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer)`
- `buf.get_mut(x, y) -> &mut Cell` — global coordinates (offset by area.x/area.y)
- `Cell::set_char(ch)`, `set_fg(Color)`, `set_bg(Color)`, `cell.modifier = Modifier::FLAGS`
- `Color::Indexed(u8)`, `Color::Rgb(u8, u8, u8)`, `Color::Reset`
- `Modifier::BOLD | DIM | ITALIC | UNDERLINED | SLOW_BLINK | REVERSED | HIDDEN | CROSSED_OUT`

## Scope

- `crates/speedrun/src/ui.rs` — new file

## Tickets

- 2.2.1 — Cell mapping and basic rendering
- 2.2.2 — Viewport and scroll offset
- 2.2.3 — Widget integration tests

title: 2.2.1 — Cell mapping and basic rendering
2.2.2 — Viewport and scroll offset
2.2.3 — Widget integration tests
notes:
## Test visibility for private helpers

The test code in this ticket calls `map_color()` and `map_modifiers()` directly. These are private functions in `ui.rs`. To make them testable, use one of these approaches:

**Recommended**: Put tests inside `ui.rs` as a `#[cfg(test)] mod tests {}` block. This gives tests access to private functions. The tests in this ticket should go inside `crates/speedrun/src/ui.rs`, not in a separate test file.

```rust
// At the bottom of ui.rs:
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use ratatui::buffer::Buffer;
    // ... all tests here have access to map_color, map_modifiers, etc.
}
```

**Alternative**: Make `map_color` and `map_modifiers` `pub(crate)` instead of private. This is acceptable since they're implementation details of the TUI crate, not part of a public library API.

## `rgb` crate import for tests

The color mapping tests use `rgb::RGB8 { r: 255, g: 128, b: 0 }`. The `rgb` crate must be available. It should already be in `Cargo.toml` (added in 2.2.1 or 2.1.1). Verify with:
```rust
use rgb::RGB8;
```

## Test data paths

Tests reference `../../testdata/minimal_v2.cast`. This path is relative to `crates/speedrun/src/` (two levels up from the source file to the workspace root, then into `testdata/`). However, `cargo test` runs from the crate root (`crates/speedrun/`), so the correct relative path is `../../testdata/minimal_v2.cast`. This matches how `speedrun-core` tests use the same pattern.

## `create_vt` is public

`speedrun_core::create_vt(cols, rows)` is re-exported from `lib.rs` and available for test usage. This is how the attribute mapping tests construct a Vt to feed SGR sequences to.

## avt dependency needed for tests

Tests import `avt::Color` for constructing test values (e.g., `avt::Color::Indexed(1)`). The `avt` dependency must be in `Cargo.toml` (should have been added in 2.2.1).

title: Epic 4.3 — UX polish and bug fixes
description:
## Summary

Fix playback bugs and polish the CLI. The progress bar appears frozen between events, Space at end-of-recording does nothing, the comma (step backward) keybind doesn't work, and the --keyframe-interval flag has no short form.

## Context

Phase 3 delivered a fully functional TUI player, but manual testing reveals several UX issues. The progress bar only redraws when events fire (frozen between events). Pressing Space when playback has ended toggles playing but immediately auto-pauses (no restart). The comma keybind for step-backward seeks to the same event that's already on screen (off-by-one). The --keyframe-interval flag is the only long flag without a short alias.

## Scope

- Smooth timeline progression (~30fps redraw during playback)
- Space at end restarts from beginning
- Fix step backward (comma keybind) off-by-one
- Add -k flag for --keyframe-interval
- Verify resize and startup/shutdown behavior

## Tickets

1. 4.3.1 — Smooth timeline + space-restart (app.rs event loop)
2. 4.3.2 — Fix comma (step backward) keybind (player.rs)
3. 4.3.3 — CLI polish + resize + startup/shutdown verification (main.rs + verification)

## Ordering

4.3.1 and 4.3.2 can run in parallel (app.rs vs player.rs). 4.3.3 after both (touches main.rs, verification touches app.rs).
close_reason: All 3 tickets completed and merged to main. Smooth timeline (~30fps redraw), space-restart at end, step-backward off-by-one fix, and -k short flag for --keyframe-interval.

title: 4.3.1 — Smooth timeline + space-restart
description:
## Summary

Two playback loop fixes in app.rs. The progress bar appears frozen between events because the TUI only redraws when events fire. Pressing Space at the end of a recording does nothing instead of restarting from the beginning.

## Bug 1: Frozen timeline

**Root cause:** The event loop in app.rs:146-156 only sets `needs_redraw = true` when `player.tick(dt)` returns `true` (i.e., when events are processed). Between events, `current_time` advances in the player but the progress bar and time display are never re-rendered. Additionally, `compute_timeout` (app.rs:177-190) sleeps until the next event via `time_to_next_event()`, so there are no wake-ups between events at all.

**Fix:**
1. In `compute_timeout`, cap the final return value to ~33ms (30fps) when `player.is_playing()`. Apply the cap as the last step — the existing auto-hide logic stays the same, then `.min(Duration::from_millis(33))` is applied to whatever the method would otherwise return, but ONLY when `self.player.is_playing()`. When paused, do not apply the cap (keep existing behavior: sleep until next interaction, up to 100ms).
2. In the tick handling block (app.rs:146-156), set `needs_redraw = true` whenever `player.is_playing()`, not just when `tick()` returns `true`. The time display and progress bar need continuous updates even when no events fire. This is safe because ratatui uses differential rendering — it diffs against the previous buffer and only writes changed cells to the terminal, so 30fps redraws when only the time/progress changed are cheap.

Concretely, modify `compute_timeout` to end with:
```rust
if self.player.is_playing() {
    base.min(Duration::from_millis(33))
} else {
    base
}
```
where `base` is whatever the method currently returns.

## Bug 2: Space at end

**Root cause:** The `TogglePlayback` handler (app.rs:221-230) calls `player.toggle()` which just flips `playing = true`. On the very next `tick()`, the player sees `current_time >= duration` and immediately sets `playing = false` (player.rs:367-370). Net effect: nothing visible happens.

**Fix:** In the `TogglePlayback` handler, BEFORE calling `player.toggle()`, add:
```rust
if !self.player.is_playing()
    && self.player.current_time() >= self.player.duration()
    && self.player.duration() > 0.0
{
    self.player.seek(0.0);
}
```
The `!is_playing()` guard is critical — without it, pressing Space to PAUSE while at the end would seek to 0 (teleporting the user to the start while they were trying to pause). The `duration() > 0.0` guard prevents an empty recording from entering a seek-then-auto-pause loop.

The `>=` comparison for at-end detection is reliable because `tick()` clamps `current_time` to `duration` via `f64::min()`, so after auto-pause `current_time` is exactly `duration`. Do not use an epsilon comparison.

## Implementation

1. Modify `compute_timeout()` to apply 33ms cap when playing (as described above).
2. In the main loop tick block, add `if self.player.is_playing() { needs_redraw = true; }` after the existing `tick()` call.
3. Add the at-end seek in `TogglePlayback` handler, before `player.toggle()`.
4. Update existing `compute_timeout` tests — there are existing tests in app.rs. The 33ms cap changes expected values when playing. Tests for the paused case should be unaffected.
5. Add new tests (see below).

Note: `needs_redraw` is a local variable in the `run()` loop and cannot be directly asserted in unit tests. The `compute_timeout` test is the proxy — verifying the wake-up frequency ensures redraws happen at 30fps.

## New tests

- `compute_timeout_capped_while_playing`: set up player with time_to_next_event returning 10 seconds, player is playing → assert `compute_timeout() <= Duration::from_millis(33)`.
- `compute_timeout_uncapped_while_paused`: same setup but player is paused → assert `compute_timeout() > Duration::from_millis(33)` (should be ~100ms or the time_to_next_event value).
- `space_at_end_restarts`: play to end (seek to duration, verify `!is_playing()` and `current_time() >= duration()`), handle `TogglePlayback` → assert `is_playing() == true` and `current_time() == 0.0`.
- `space_on_empty_recording`: load empty.cast, handle `TogglePlayback` → assert no panic, player toggles normally without seeking.

## Files touched

- `crates/speedrun/src/app.rs` — compute_timeout, event loop tick block, TogglePlayback handler, tests
acceptance_criteria:
- During playback, the progress bar and time display update smoothly (~30fps), not just on event boundaries.
- compute_timeout never exceeds 33ms while playing (verified by test).
- compute_timeout is unaffected (up to 100ms or time_to_next_event) while paused (verified by test).
- Pressing Space when playback has ended and player is paused restarts from the beginning (current_time resets to 0, playback resumes).
- The !is_playing() guard prevents seek-to-0 when pressing Space to pause at the end.
- Pressing Space on a zero-duration recording toggles without seeking or crashing.
- All existing app tests pass.
- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes.
- cargo fmt --check passes.

title: 4.3.2 — Fix comma (step backward) keybind
acceptance_criteria:
- Pressing comma while paused visibly changes the terminal content to the previous output event's state (verified by screen content assertion, not just time).
- Pressing comma repeatedly walks backward through output events, one per press (verified by strictly decreasing current_time across presses).
- Pressing comma when at or before the first output event does nothing (returns false).
- Step forward then step backward round-trips to the same screen content.
- Step backward after tick() (play → pause → comma) changes screen content.
- The playing guard is preserved: step_backward returns false when playing.
- All existing step tests pass (with updated assertions where needed).
- cargo test --workspace passes.

- cargo fmt --check passes.

title: 4.3.3 — CLI polish + resize + startup/shutdown verification
description:
## Summary

Add the `-k` short flag for `--keyframe-interval`, and verify terminal resize handling and clean startup/shutdown in all exit paths.

## Context

All other long flags have short aliases (`-s` for speed, `-t` for start-at, `-i` for idle-limit) but `--keyframe-interval` does not. Terminal resize (`Event::Resize`) is already handled at app.rs:136-138 (sets `needs_redraw = true`). Startup/shutdown (panic hook at main.rs:63-71, `restore_terminal` at main.rs:117) is already implemented. This ticket is mostly a one-line code change plus verification work that is primarily for human review.

## Implementation

1. In `main.rs:37`, change `#[arg(long, default_value_t = 5.0)]` to `#[arg(short = 'k', long, default_value_t = 5.0)]`.

2. Verify (by code inspection) that `Event::Resize` at app.rs:136-138 sets `needs_redraw = true`. Note: automated testing of resize is not feasible — the event loop (`app.run()`) is blocking, and crossterm events cannot be injected from unit tests. The existing test infrastructure uses `handle_action()` which bypasses the event loop, and resize is handled at the event level, not the action level.

3. Verify (by code inspection) startup/shutdown:
   - Startup: first frame renders before entering event loop (app.rs renders immediately).
   - Shutdown: `restore_terminal()` runs in all exit paths (main.rs:117 via `let _` pattern).
   - Panic: panic hook (main.rs:63-71) restores raw mode and alternate screen.

4. Create a manual test checklist and document in the PR:
   - [ ] Resize during playback — re-renders cleanly, no artifacts
   - [ ] Resize while paused — re-renders cleanly
   - [ ] Ctrl-C during playback — terminal restored
   - [ ] Quit via q — terminal restored
   - [ ] Quit via Esc — terminal restored
   - [ ] `cargo run -- -k 2 testdata/real_session.cast` — plays correctly
   - [ ] `-k` appears in `--help` output

## Notes

- The automated deliverable is the `-k` flag (one line). The manual checklist is for human review during PR approval, not for the agent to execute.
- This ticket touches `main.rs` which is also touched by sfs.2 (stdin support). If both are in flight on parallel branches, expect a trivial merge conflict in the Args struct (different fields).

## Files touched
